title: Configure Default Memory Requests and Limits for a Namespace — What if you specify a container's request, but not its limit?
text:
Here's a manifest for a Pod that has one container. The container
specifies a memory request, but not a limit:
apiVersion
:
v1
kind
:
Pod
metadata
:
name
:
default-mem-demo-3
spec
:
containers
:
-
name
:
default-mem-demo-3-ctr
image
:
nginx
resources
:
requests
:
memory
:
"128Mi"
Create the Pod:
kubectl apply -f https://k8s.io/examples/admin/resource/memory-defaults-pod-3.yaml --namespace
=
default-mem-example
View the Pod's specification:
kubectl get pod default-mem-demo-3 --output
=
yaml --namespace
=
default-mem-example
The output shows that the container's memory request is set to the value specified in the
container's manifest. The container is limited to use no more than 512MiB of
memory, which matches the default memory limit for the namespace.
resources:
  limits:
    memory: 512Mi
  requests:
    memory: 128Mi
